name: GastroGuard
tagline: GI symptom tracking and personalized insight engine
summary: Tracks GI symptoms, meals, and triggers and builds insight over time. Designed to identify real cause-effect patterns.
useCase: Used to track how diet and behavior drive GI flare-ups.

capabilityDetails.fullstack: Next.js PWA with Supabase auth and structured GI logging.
capabilityDetails.data-analysis: Trigger scoring and longitudinal patterns on symptom data.

name: MindMap+
tagline: Mental state tracking with behavioral pattern recognition
summary: Captures mental state as structured data. Turns mood, stress, and behavior into patterns you can analyze.
useCase: Used to understand how behavior and environment affect mood and focus.

capabilityDetails.fullstack: Next.js app with Supabase and Recharts dashboards.
capabilityDetails.data-analysis: Check-ins turned into structured time-series behavioral signals.

name: SkinTrack+
tagline: Lesion and skin condition tracking with environmental correlation
summary: Tracks skin conditions over time and ties them to environmental and behavioral inputs. Built to identify patterns, not just log events.
useCase: Used to find what actually drives flare-ups.

capabilityDetails.data-analysis: Correlates lesions and breakouts with diet, sun, and stress.
capabilityDetails.biomedical-embedded: Vision pipeline plus physiological context for skin lesions.

name: GlucoLoop
summary: Builds a tighter feedback loop on top of CGM data. Focused on faster and more actionable glucose insight.
useCase: Used to respond to glucose changes in real time.

capabilityDetails.fullstack: FastAPI services and Supabase backing CGM analytics APIs.
capabilityDetails.data-analysis: Faster, more actionable glucose insights from CGM streams.

name: HealthHelper
tagline: Unified health data layer and cross-app analytics engine
summary: Acts as the central data layer across all projects. Connects separate health signals into one system.
useCase: Used to analyze relationships between sleep, stress, GI, and behavior.

capabilityDetails.fullstack: Unified Postgres and Supabase layer across HeartWire apps.
capabilityDetails.data-analysis: Cross-domain analytics across sleep, GI, stress, and behavior.

name: HeartWire OS
tagline: Academic and engineering operating system for structured study and long-term learning
summary: A structured system for studying, planning, and building. Designed for long-term learning, not short-term productivity.
useCase: Used to organize work, track learning, and manage projects over time.

capabilityDetails.tools-workflow: Study OS for notes, plans, and long-horizon engineering depth.
capabilityDetails.fullstack: Next.js workspace for structured self-education over time.

name: Modular Knee Brace
tagline: Adaptive biomechanical support system for knee stability and recovery
summary: A modular knee brace focused on stability, load distribution, and real-world use. Designed to adapt to different recovery needs.
useCase: Used to support joint stability during training and recovery.
imageAlt: CAD render of the modular knee brace, isometric view

capabilityDetails.biomedical-embedded: CAD, printing, and biomechanics for wearable joint support.
capabilityDetails.core-engineering: Load sharing and alignment across modular brace components.

detail.problem: Knee instability and pain often come from support that is either too generic or too rigid. Off-the-shelf braces rarely adapt to changing swelling, activity level, or limb geometry, so users trade comfort for stability—or the opposite.
detail.design: The system is built from modular components and an adjustable structure so fit and support can be tuned without replacing the entire device. The goal is repeatable alignment and load sharing across sessions, not a single fixed geometry.
engineering: Force distribution: spread joint-adjacent loads across larger surface areas to reduce pressure hot spots and material fatigue.
Joint alignment: maintain consistent hinge axis and varus/valgus control relative to the femoral-tibial line during flexion.
Comfort vs rigidity: calibrate strapping tension and segment stiffness so the brace stays worn during real activity—under-support fails clinically; over-support fails adherence.

name: JonnyJr
summary: Automates research and turns raw information into structured outputs. Built as a personal research engine.
useCase: Used to speed up research, synthesis, and documentation.

capabilityDetails.ai-automation: OpenAI and GitHub Actions for research synthesis pipelines.
capabilityDetails.tools-workflow: Structured automation outputs spanning engineering projects.

name: Stryker ReUnion RSA Teardown
tagline: Reverse-engineering a Class II reverse shoulder arthroplasty system end-to-end.
summary: A full-system teardown of a reverse shoulder implant: disease state → biomechanics → requirements → regulatory → manufacturing → outcomes.
useCase: Built to be a repeatable benchmarking framework for future device design, not just documentation of one implant.

capabilityDetails.biomedical-embedded: Device biomechanics + materials system mapped to clinical requirements and failure modes.
capabilityDetails.core-engineering: Benchmarking method: constraints → tradeoffs → decisions across the device lifecycle.

sections.title: What it is
paragraphs: Stryker's ReUnion RSA is a Class II reverse shoulder arthroplasty system, indicated for patients with rotator cuff tear arthropathy in which the native joint no longer has the soft-tissue stabilizers required for a conventional total shoulder replacement.

This project reverse-engineers the device end to end: the underlying disease state, the geometric inversion that allows the deltoid to compensate for a non-functional rotator cuff, the materials system (Ti-6Al-4V, Tritanium 3D porous metal, and X3 highly crosslinked UHMWPE), the FDA 510(k) clearance pathway, the Quality Function Deployment targets, and the manufacturing and Design for Environment decisions that support them.

sections.title: Objective
paragraphs: The objective was not to document the implant, but to develop a repeatable benchmarking framework — disease state → biomechanics → requirements → regulatory → manufacturing → outcomes — that can be applied in reverse to the design of future medical devices.

name: Engineering the Healthcare Supply Chain
tagline: 12 interviews with healthcare professionals on where engineering fails (and what clinicians actually need).
summary: Primary qualitative research with 12 healthcare professionals (8 physicians) on clinical risk, supply fragility, and the clinical–engineering gap.
useCase: Used to translate clinician demand signals into engineering targets: reliability, usability, data fluency, and cross-domain communication.

capabilityDetails.core-engineering: Systems framing of failure modes and operational constraints in clinical environments.
capabilityDetails.tools-workflow: Interview protocol → synthesis → recommendations as a repeatable research workflow.

sections.title: Why it mattered
paragraphs: Most engineers never spend structured time inside a clinical environment. This case study changed that.

Through 12 interviews with healthcare professionals — 8 of them practicing physicians — I examined how engineers shape daily patient care, where supply chain breakdowns create real clinical risk, and what biomedical engineers need to understand before they ever touch a device or system.

sections.title: Findings
paragraphs: The findings revealed consistent demand signals: reliable supply infrastructure, usable device design, data fluency, and the rare ability to communicate across the clinical-engineering divide.

name: Co–Cr Dental Prosthesis Degradation Review
tagline: A PROSPERO-registered systematic review on material degradation outcomes.
summary: Pulled and synthesized 11 studies on corrosion, wear, and ion release in cobalt-chromium dental prostheses under PRISMA 2020.
useCase: Used to isolate which controllable variables most strongly drive clinical degradation outcomes.

capabilityDetails.data-analysis: Cross-study synthesis: corrosion resistance, wear behavior, and ion release outcomes.
capabilityDetails.core-engineering: Key driver identification to inform upstream design and manufacturing decisions.

sections.title: Overview
paragraphs: A PROSPERO-registered systematic review on material degradation in cobalt-chromium dental prostheses.
Pulled and synthesized 11 studies across PubMed, Scopus, and Web of Science under PRISMA 2020 — covering corrosion resistance, wear behavior, and ion release.

sections.title: Key finding
paragraphs: The finding that stood out: surface finishing drives clinical degradation outcomes more than alloy composition or how the prosthesis was fabricated.
